lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Hello [player name]! Thanks again for your help with the dig.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Bartak used the design of the shield you extracted to recreate a shield like it. Even though the original shield was poorly made, the design itself was a good one.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Perhaps there is some important knowledge that we have lost since the early, more war-like days of Dorgesh-Kaan.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: The helmet you helped to unearth has come in useful. I used it as a visual aid when I went to talk to the children in the nursery about our history.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: It's amazing that the ancient goblins' eyes were even smaller than a child's!

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Oldak examined the axe blade you dug up and found that it had a faint magical aura. It looks like the mace wasn't the only magical weapon our ancestors brought from the surface.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: In fact, it could be that a lot of their equipment was magical. This means that either the ancient goblins were skilled magical craftsmen, or some other power was supplying them with magical equipment.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: We used the armour fragment you unearthed to reconstruct the shape of the original armour, and with it the shape of the goblin who wore the armour.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: This told us a lot about how the goblin body shape changed from its surface form to that of the cave goblins.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: When we examined the sword you dug up, we found that the blade is covered in tiny symbols.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: The scribe thinks he has enough of them to begin understanding the ancient goblin language, but it will take a while for him to decipher them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost the ancient goblin mace.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: I know. Zanik says she found it lying around in the caves near the city, almost as if it had made its way back here and wanted her to find it.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: I know. We found it lying around in the caves near the city, almost as if it had made its way back here and wanted to be found.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: If you want it back, I will sell it to you for 1,000 coins. That should discourage you from losing it again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I'll have to go and get the money.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I should free up some inventory space first.

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Look after it well. All the goblins who have handled it say it made them feel uneasy. We want it kept safe, but we don't want it near the city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way!

lines.speaker: Tegdak
lines.text: Suit yourself.
